steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank appears between the subject “Wisconsin,” and the main verb “reaches,” with “1,372 feet in elevation” as the object of the blank. The structure requires a word type that allows the following phrase to modify the subject without creating a second independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
A present participle forms a participial phrase that modifies the subject. Spanning “1,372 feet in elevation” correctly modifies Wisconsin when set off by commas.

The finite verbs (spans, span) would create a comma splice, and spanned cannot function as a participial modifier.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
